ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR DEFENDANTS WILLIS INSURANCE SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC. AND WILLIS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT. 301 PER CHALLENGE TO JUDGE BUSCH) HEARING SET FOR JUN-06-2007 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLIS INSURANCE SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC. AND WILLIS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED BASED ON ARMENDARIZ. THE CONTRACT IS ONE OF ADHESION. TO EFFECT THE ACQUISITION, PLAINTIFF WAS REQUIRED TO SIGN IT OR BE PRECLUDED FROM WORKING IN THE INSURANCE INDUSTRY FOR THREE YEARS. THERE ARE SEVERAL SUBSTANTIVE LIMITATIONS IN THE AGREEMENT: ELIMINATING INJUNCTIVE ACTIONS BY THE EMPLOYER (PARAGRAPH SIX); LIMITING PUNITIVE DAMAGES (PARAGRAPH 6F); REQUIRING THE EMPLOYEE TO BEAR HALF OF THE COSTS OF ARBITRATION EXCEPT IN LIMITED CASES (PARAGRAPH 6H); AND LIMITING DISCOVERY BY REQUIRING THAT THE ARBITRATION BE COMPLETED WITHIN NINETY DAYS. SEVERANCE WILL NOT CURE THE MULTIPLE LIMITATIONS IN THE AGREEMENT. MS. SMALLETS IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: GENA EALES, CSR 	176

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash The Subpoenas To Peter Navolanic, M.D. And Academy Of Arts College And Memo Of P&A And Declaration Of Sonya Smallets In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Sep-17-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:


Appearance:
Sonya Smallets, Esq., for Ronald Dial, Plaintiff/Moving Party
Paula A. Klemens, Esq., for Willis Insurance Services of California, Inc., Defendant/Opposing Party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Ronald Dial's Motion To Quash The Subpoenas To Peter Navolanic, M.D. and Academy Of Arts College is granted in part and denied in part.
As to the subpoena to Peter Navolanic, M.D., the court orders that the subpoena is limited to records relating to meningitis, for the period January 1, 2002 to present, relating to conditions alleged to have been caused or exacerbated by Defendants. The parties shall share equally the costs of a third party review..
As to the subpoena to the Academy Of Arts College, the motion is granted. There is a right to privacy to school records, and the subpoena is overly broad as it seeks records which are not directly relevant and for which there is no good cause to obtain. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2007 Continued To Oct-19-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Defendants Willis Insurance Services Of California, Inc. And Willis North America, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Jun-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WILLIS INSURANCE SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC. AND WILLIS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION.
APPEARANCES: SONYA SMALLETS, ESQUIRE (LAWLESS & LAWLESS, 415.391.7555) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MARGARET J. GROVER, ESQUIRE (HAIGHT BROWN & BONESTEEL, 415.646.7500) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED BASED ON ARMENDARIZ. THE CONTRACT IS ONE OF ADHESION. TO EFFECT THE ACQUISITION, PLAINTIFF WAS REQUIRED TO SIGN IT OR BE PRECLUDED FROM WORKING IN THE INSURANCE INDUSTRY FOR THREE YEARS. THERE ARE SEVERAL SUBSTANTIVE LIMITATIONS IN THE AGREEMENT: ELIMINATING INJUNCTIVE ACTIONS BY THE EMPLOYER (PARAGRAPH SIX); LIMITING PUNITIVE DAMAGES (PARAGRAPH 6F); REQUIRING THE EMPLOYEE TO BEAR HALF OF THE COSTS OF ARBITRATION EXCEPT IN LIMITED CASES (PARAGRAPH 6H); AND LIMITING DISCOVERY BY REQUIRING THAT THE ARBITRATION BE COMPLETED WITHIN NINETY DAYS. SEVERANCE WILL NOT CURE THE MULTIPLE LIMITATIONS IN THE AGREEMENT. MS. SMALLETS IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: GENA EALES, CSR 9176
